page: Google Cloud Platform n4a-standard-48 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the n4a server family. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs and 192.0 GB of system memory, maintaining a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio with 4.0 GB of memory allocated per core. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage options for persistent disk requirements.

The dedicated CPU allocation ensures that the 48 vCPUs deliver consistent compute performance without resource contention from other tenant workloads. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the standard resource density of the n4a-standard-48 provides a balanced allocation of compute and memory resources, making it suitable for enterprise workloads that require predictable performance without the premium cost associated with specialized memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated hardware.

This server configuration is designed for general-purpose workloads. Appropriate use cases include medium-to-large web serving fleets, enterprise application hosting, containerized microservices, build systems, and database nodes that do not require high-performance local NVMe storage or specialized hardware acceleration.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4a-standard-48 is a general-purpose server instance configured with 48 dedicated vCPUs and 192.0 GB of system memory on the x86_64 architecture. Part of the n4a server family, this instance maintains a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, providing 4.0 GB of memory per core. It does not feature local storage or GPU accelerators, meaning all storage must be provisioned via network-attached volumes. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures stable, predictable performance for multi-threaded applications. Qualitatively, the n4a-standard-48 offers a balanced resource profile, making it a cost-efficient choice for workloads that do not require specialized hardware. It is well-suited for enterprise application hosting, web serving, container orchestration, and general-purpose business applications.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n4a-standard-48 is an x86_64 general-purpose server featuring 48 dedicated vCPUs and 192 GB of RAM, designed for balanced enterprise workloads without local storage.

tagline: A balanced x86_64 server configuration featuring forty-eight dedicated vCPUs and one hundred ninety-two gigabytes of memory for general-purpose enterprise workloads.

Provides 192.0 GB of system memory with a 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
No integrated GPU or local storage included

Suited for enterprise applications, web serving, and containerized workloads
